COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or an objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-26-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
Lisa Edgar-Dickman, Esq. appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to NOV-20-2018 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Judge: TERI L. JACKSON; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-18-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Aug-01-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2018 Continued To Jun-27-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Noel E. Macaulay, Esq. of Schwartz & Janzen, LLP, T:310.979.4090 for defendants/moving party, on Court Call;
Lisa Edgar-Dickman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Lisa Edgar Dickman, T:925.945.1045, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modifies the tentative ruling, allowing plaintiff leave to amend, as follows:

DEFENDANTS SUHEIL SHATARA and SHATARA ARCHITECTURE, INC.'s DEMURRER to all causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff RH Investments, Inc. is sustained as to all causes of action, with 10 days leave to file a 2nd Amended Certificate Of Merit.

Counsel for defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2018 Continued To Apr-25-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
